intro: Rewriting Rust in Python prioritises developer ergonomics and iteration speed over Rust's zero-cost abstractions and memory-safety guarantees. Rust's ownership and lifetimes disappear — Python's garbage collector handles memory automatically. Rust's `Result` and `Option` types map to Python's exception handling and `None`. The tradeoff is reduced performance and no compile-time memory safety, but significantly faster development and a much shorter codebase.

diffs.from: Ownership + borrow checker. No GC. Compile-time lifetime enforcement.
diffs.to: Garbage collected (reference counting). No manual memory management.

diffs.from: Result<T,E> and Option<T> as return values. ? operator for propagation.
diffs.to: Exceptions. try/except at boundaries. No checked errors.

diffs.from: Native code, zero-cost abstractions. Predictable low latency.
diffs.to: Interpreted; CPython GIL limits CPU parallelism. ~10–100x slower.

diffs.from: Explicit ownership annotations, lifetimes, and trait bounds.
diffs.to: Terse. Variables are dynamic. No lifetime annotations.

diffs.from: Fearless concurrency: send/sync traits prevent data races at compile time.
diffs.to: GIL prevents true thread parallelism. Use multiprocessing for CPU parallelism.

examples.title: Result → exception
examples.description: Rust's match on Result translates to Python's try/except with the same branching logic.

examples.title: Struct + impl → class
examples.description: Rust structs with impl blocks map directly to Python classes. Drop ownership annotations entirely.

tips.title: Drop ownership annotations
tips.body: Remove all lifetime annotations, &, &mut, and ownership transfers. Python's GC handles all of that. Variables are freely sharable without borrow constraints.

tips.title: Result/Option → exceptions/None
tips.body: Replace Ok(val)/Err(e) returns with plain returns and raise. Replace Option<T> with returning None directly. Match on Result becomes try/except.

tips.title: Vec → list, HashMap → dict
tips.body: vec![1,2,3] becomes [1,2,3]. HashMap::new() becomes {}. push/insert become .append()/{} assignment. iter().filter().collect() becomes list comprehensions.

tips.title: Traits → duck typing or Protocol
tips.body: Rust trait bounds (T: Display + Clone) become Python duck typing — just call the method. For explicit contracts, use typing.Protocol.
